requirements.full_sentence:
The banks undertaking PD activities departmentally should follow the extant guidelines
applicable to the banks on capital adequacy requirements and risk management.
requirements.object: applicable to the banks on capital adequacy requirements and risk management

requirements.full_sentence: • Should be registered as an NBFC under Section 45-IA of the RBI Act, 1934 for at least one year prior to the submission of application.
requirements.object: NBFC under Section 45-IA of the RBI Act, 1934 for at least one year prior to the submission of application

requirements.full_sentence: • Should have minimum net owned funds (NOF)1 of Rs.150 crore for undertaking core activities and Rs.250 crore for undertaking diversified activities (as defined in para 5).
requirements.object: funds (NOF)1 of Rs

requirements.full_sentence: • Should have exposure in the securities business and in particular to the G-Sec market for at least one year prior to the submission of application.
requirements.object: securities business and in particular to the G-Sec market for at least one year prior to the submission of application

requirements.full_sentence: Applicant's turnover in the G-Sec business during the year preceding the year of application for PD authorisation should be at least equal to 15 per cent of its total turnover.
requirements.object: to 15 per cent of its total turnover

requirements.full_sentence: Its assets in G-Sec during the year preceding the year of application should be at least equal to 15 per cent of its total assets.
requirements.object: to 15 per cent of its total assets

requirements.full_sentence: • Applicant entity should submit an annual target along with plan of action for turnover to be achieved on behalf of mid- segment (Provident Funds, Urban Cooperative banks, Regional Rural banks, Trusts, etc.) and retail (individual) investors at the time of submission of their application for PD authorisation.
requirements.object: along with plan of action for turnover to be achieved on behalf of mid- segment (Provident Funds, Urban Cooperative banks, Reg

conditions: In case of subsidiaries/joint ventures set up by entities incorporated abroad
requirements.full_sentence:
• In case of subsidiaries/joint ventures set up by entities incorporated abroad, in addition to conditions given above, the following may be adhered to:
i.
The applicant should have approval of Foreign Investment Promotion Board.
requirements.object: Investment Promotion Board

requirements.full_sentence: The parent foreign company directly or through its subsidiaries should have been in PD business for three years or more in active markets.
requirements.object: business for three years or more in active markets

requirements.full_sentence:
Such entity should suitably ring fence its system from its parent and associates Banks which do not have a partly or wholly owned subsidiary undertaking PD business and intending to undertake PD business departmentally
• Minimum net owned funds (NOF) of Rs.
requirements.object: system from its parent and associates Banks which do not have a partly or wholly owned subsidiary undertaking PD business and

requirements.full_sentence: • The applicant bank should have approval of Department of Banking Regulation, Central Office, Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai.
requirements.object: of Banking Regulation, Central Office, Reserve Bank of India, Mumbai

requirements.full_sentence: • Applicant bank should submit an annual target along with plan of action for turnover to be achieved on behalf of mid- segment and retail investors at the time of submission of their application for PD authorisation.
requirements.object: along with plan of action for turnover to be achieved on behalf of mid- segment and retail investors at the time of submission

requirements.full_sentence:
In making such
determination, RBI may consult with the appropriate regulators for their views.
requirements.object: regulators for their views

requirements.full_sentence:
1.2.3 An entity satisfying the criteria stipulated above should submit its application to the Chief
General Manager, Internal Debt Management Department (IDMD), RBI, Mumbai.
requirements.object: the Chief General Manager, Internal Debt Management Department (IDMD), RBI, Mumbai

requirements.full_sentence:
1.2.5 The applicant entity may also have to adhere to other terms and conditions, as may be
specified by RBI from time-to-time.
requirements.object: to other terms and conditions, as may be specified by RBI from time-to-time

requirements.full_sentence:
1.2.6 Existing PDs should submit an annual target (in consultation with RBI) along with plan of
action for turnover to be achieved on behalf of mid-segment and retail investors in the
month of June every year.
requirements.object: (in consultation with RBI) along with plan of action for turnover to be achieved on behalf of mid-segment and retail investors

requirements.full_sentence:
The annual target for July 2016-June 2017 should not be less
than 150 per cent of minimum NOF for standalone PDs and 100 per cent of minimum
NOF for bank PDs.
requirements.object: per cent of minimum NOF for standalone PDs and 100 per cent of minimum NOF for bank PDs

requirements.full_sentence:
A PD is required to have a standing arrangement with RBI based on the execution of an
undertaking (Annex I) and the authorization letter issued by RBI every three years.
requirements.object: arrangement with RBI based on the execution of an undertaking (Annex I) and the authorization letter issued by RBI every three year

requirements.full_sentence:
1.5.1 Returns: As part of off-site supervision, PDs are required to submit prescribed periodic
returns to RBI as per Annex II-A & B.
requirements.object: returns to RBI as per Annex II-A & B

requirements.full_sentence:
RBI may also prescribe any other return as it may deem
necessary.
requirements.object: return as it may deem necessary

requirements.full_sentence: PDs are required to make available all such documents, records, etc.
requirements.object: such documents, records, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
1.5.4 In addition, PDs are required to meet registration and such other requirements as
stipulated by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) including operations on the
Stock Exchanges, if they undertake any activity regulated by SEBI.
requirements.object: such other requirements as stipulated by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) including operations on the Stock Exchanges

requirements.full_sentence:
1.5.5 A PD should bring to the attention of RBI any major complaint against it or action
initiated/taken against it by authorities such as the Stock Exchanges, SEBI, CBI, Enforcement
Directorate, Income Tax Department, etc.
requirements.object: of RBI any major complaint against it or action initiated/taken against it by authorities such as the Stock Exchanges, SEBI, CB

requirements.full_sentence:
In the GOI securities auction, a PD should bid for an amount not less than its total
underwriting obligation.
requirements.object: not less than its total underwriting obligation

requirements.full_sentence:
On announcement of an auction of State Development Loans (SDLs), which are dated
securities of the State Governments, RBI may invite PDs to collectively bid to underwrite
up to 100 per cent of the notified amount.
requirements.object: bid to underwrite up to 100 per cent of the notified amount

conditions:
if a PD fails to submit the required minimum bid or submits
a bid lower than its commitment
requirements.full_sentence:
In any auction of T-Bills/CMBs, if a PD fails to submit the required minimum bid or submits
a bid lower than its commitment, the RBI may take appropriate penal action against the
PD.
requirements.object: against the PD

requirements.full_sentence:
A PD would be required to achieve a minimum success ratio of 40 percent of bidding
commitment in T-Bills/CMBs auctions (if an auction gets cancelled or if all bids are
rejected by RBI then PD should not take that auction's notified amount in the calculation of
success ratio.
requirements.object: amount in the calculation of success ratio

requirements.full_sentence:
Also, if RBI accepts partial amount, PD should take only accepted amount
for calculation purpose) which will be monitored on a half yearly basis, i.e.
requirements.object: for calculation purpose) which will be monitored on a half yearly basis, i

requirements.full_sentence: The CMB transactions may be reported in PDR returns along with the T-Bill transactions.
requirements.object: returns along with the T-Bill transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
2.3 'When-Issued' transactions in Central G-Sec
PDs shall adhere to the guidelines issued by the RBI vide circular IDMD.No/3426/11.01.01
(D)/2005-06 dated May 3, 2006, as updated vide circulars IDMD.No/2130/11/01.01 (D)/2006-07
dated November 16, 2006, IDMD.DOD.No.3166/11/01.01(B)/2007-08 dated January 1, 2008
and FMRD.DIRD.06/14.03.07/2015-16 dated December 10, 2015 for undertaking "When
Issued" transactions.
requirements.object: issued by the RBI vide circular IDMD

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should have a Board approved policy including appropriate risk management
system to take care of risks arising out of such activity
ii.
requirements.object: policy including appropriate risk management system to take care of risks arising out of such activity ii

requirements.full_sentence:
List of clients, details of business done through such clients and appropriateness of risk
management system put in place may be periodically reviewed by the Board.
requirements.object: the Board

requirements.full_sentence: Records maintained in this regard should be subjected to concurrent audit.
requirements.object: audit

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs may execute the secondary market sale transaction and report the same on NDS-OM (T+1
settlement).
requirements.object: sale transaction and report the same on NDS-OM (T+1 settlement)

requirements.full_sentence:
With respect to the dated securities, the sale transaction may be made within one
hour of intimation of firm allotment in the primary auctions.
requirements.object: hour of intimation of firm allotment in the primary auctions

requirements.full_sentence:
2.5 Submission of non-competitive bids
PDs shall adhere to the guidelines issued vide circular RBI/2008-09/479 - IDMD.No.5877/
08.02.33/2008-09 dated May 22, 2009, as amended from time to time, in respect of submission
of non-competitive bids in the auctions of the G-Sec.
requirements.object: issued vide circular RBI/2008-09/479 - IDMD

requirements.full_sentence:
2.6 Sale of securities allotted in primary issues on the same day
PDs shall adhere to the guidelines issued vide circulars IDMC.PDRS.No.PDS.1/03.64.00/ 2000-
01 dated October 6, 2000, RBI/2005/461-IDMD.PDRS.4777/10.02.01/2004-05 dated May 11,
2005 and RBI/2012-13/133-IDMD.PDRD.188/03.64.00/2012-13 dated July 16, 2012 for
undertaking sale of securities allotted in primary issues on the same day.
requirements.object: issued vide circulars IDMC

requirements.full_sentence:
Successful PDs shall provide sufficient funds in their
current account with the RBI on the auction settlement days before 3:00 pm to meet their
obligations against the subscriptions in the primary auctions failing which the shortage will be
treated as an instance of 'SGL bouncing' and will be subjected to the applicable penal
provisions.
requirements.object: their current account with the RBI on the auction settlement days before 3:00 pm to meet their obligations against the sub

requirements.full_sentence:
3.1 Market making in G-Sec: PDs should offer two-way prices in G-Sec through the Negotiated
Dealing System-Order Matching (NDS-OM), over-the-counter (OTC) market and recognized
Stock Exchanges in India and take principal positions in the secondary market for G-Sec.
requirements.object: G-Sec through the Negotiated Dealing System-Order Matching (NDS-OM), over-the-counter (OTC) market and recognized Stock Exchan

requirements.full_sentence:
3.2 Turnover ratio: A PD should annually achieve a minimum turnover ratio of 5 times for
Government dated securities and 10 times for T-Bills/CMBs of the average month-end stocks.
requirements.object: turnover ratio of 5 times for Government dated securities and 10 times for T-Bills/CMBs of the average month-end stocks

requirements.full_sentence:
The turnover ratio in respect of outright transactions should not be less than 3 times in
Government dated securities and 6 times in T-Bills/CMBs (Turnover ratio is the ratio of total
purchase and sales during the year in the secondary market to average month-end stocks).
requirements.object: times in Government dated securities and 6 times in T-Bills/CMBs (Turnover ratio is the ratio of total purchase and sales during the ye

requirements.full_sentence:
3.3 Secondary Market Transactions - Short-selling
PDs shall adhere to the guidelines issued by the RBI vide circular IDMD.No
03/11.01.01(B)/2005-06 dated February 28, 2006, RBI/2006-07/243-
IDMD.No./11.01.01(B)/2006-07 dated January 31, 2007, IDMD.DOD.No.3165 /11.01.01(B)/
2007-08 dated January 2008, IDMD.PCD.
requirements.object: issued by the RBI vide circular IDMD

requirements.full_sentence:
3.4 Separate Trading of Registered Interest and Principal of Securities (STRIPS) in G-Sec
PDs shall adhere to the guidelines issued by the RBI vide circular RBI/2009-10/360-
IDMD.DOD.No.7/11.01.09 /2009-10 dated March 25, 2010, on STRIPS in G-Sec, as amended
from time to time.
requirements.object: issued by the RBI vide circular RBI/2009-10/360- IDMD

requirements.full_sentence:
3.5 Settlement of Secondary Market transaction
As per circular IDMD.PDRS./4783/10.02.01/2004-05 dated May 11, 2005 all outright secondary
market transactions in Government Securities are settled on T+1 basis except for outright
secondary market transactions undertaken with respect to FPIs and reported on NDS-OM,
which will be settled on T+2 basis subject to conditions mentioned in circular
FMRD.DIRD.06/14.03.007/2014-15 dated March 20, 2015, as amended from time to time.
requirements.object: secondary market transactions in Government Securities are settled on T+1 basis except for outright secondary market transaction

requirements.full_sentence:
5.1 Investment policy - PDs should frame and implement, a Board approved, investment policy
and operational guidelines on securities transactions.
requirements.object: Board approved, investment policy and operational guidelines on securities transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy should contain the broad
objectives to be followed while undertaking transactions in securities on their own account and
on behalf of clients, clearly define the authority to put through deals, and lay down procedure to
be followed while putting through deals, various prudential exposure limits, policy regarding
dealings through brokers, systems for management of various risks, guidelines for valuation of
the portfolio and the reporting systems etc.
requirements.object: to be followed while undertaking transactions in securities on their own account and on behalf of clients, clearly define

requirements.full_sentence:
Operational procedures and controls in relation to
the day-to-day business operations should also be worked out and put in place to ensure that
operations in securities are conducted in accordance with sound and acceptable business
practices.
requirements.object: and put in place to ensure that operations in securities are conducted in accordance with sound and acceptable business practices

requirements.full_sentence:
While laying down these guidelines, the PDs should strictly adhere to RBI's
instructions, issued from time to time.
requirements.object: instructions, issued from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
The effectiveness of the policy and operational guidelines
should be periodically evaluated.

5.2 The investment in G-Sec should have predominance over the non-core activities in terms of
investment pattern.
requirements.object: non-core activities in terms of investment pattern

requirements.full_sentence:
Standalone PDs are required to ensure predominance by maintaining at
least 50 per cent of their total financial investments (both long term and short term) in G-Sec at
any point of time.
requirements.object: maintaining at least 50 per cent of their total financial investments (both long term and short term) in G-Sec at any point

requirements.full_sentence:
5.3 Further, a PD's investment in G-Sec (including T-Bills and CMBs) and Corporate Bond (to
the extent of 50% of NOF) on a daily basis should be at least equal to its net call/notice/repo
(including CBLO) borrowing plus net RBI borrowing (through LAF/ Intra-Day Liquidity/ Liquidity
Support) plus the minimum prescribed NOF.
requirements.object: to its net call/notice/repo (including CBLO) borrowing plus net RBI borrowing (through LAF/ Intra-Day Liquidity/ Liquidity Support)

requirements.full_sentence: 5.4 PDs should necessarily hold their investments in G-Sec portfolio in SGL with RBI.
requirements.object: in G-Sec portfolio in SGL with RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
They may
also have a dematerialised (Demat) account with depositories - National Securities Depository
Limited / Central Depository Services (India) Limited.
requirements.object: (Demat) account with depositories - National Securities Depository Limited / Central Depository Services (India) Limited

requirements.full_sentence:
All purchase/sale transactions in G-Secs
by PDs should be through SGL / Constituent SGL (CSGL) / Demat accounts.
requirements.object: Constituent SGL (CSGL) / Demat accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
5.5 PDs should hold all other investments such as CPs, bonds and debentures (privately placed
or otherwise) and equity instruments, only in demat form.
requirements.object: such as CPs, bonds and debentures (privately placed or otherwise) and equity instruments, only in demat form

requirements.full_sentence:
5.6 All problem exposures, which are not backed by any security or backed by security of
doubtful value, should be fully provided for.

conditions:
Where a PD has filed suit against another party for
recovery
requirements.full_sentence:
Where a PD has filed suit against another party for
recovery, such exposures should be evaluated and provisions made to the satisfaction of
auditors.
requirements.object: made to the satisfaction of auditors

requirements.full_sentence:
Any claim against the PD should also be taken note of and provisions made to the
satisfaction of auditors.
requirements.object: of and provisions made to the satisfaction of auditors

conditions:
if any, and also the effect
of valuation of portfolio
requirements.full_sentence:
5.7 The profit and loss account should reflect the problem exposures if any, and also the effect
of valuation of portfolio, as per the instructions issued by the RBI, from time to time.
requirements.object: if any, and also the effect of valuation of portfolio, as per the instructions issued by the RBI, from time to time

requirements.full_sentence:
The report
of the statutory auditors should contain a certification to this effect.
requirements.object: this effect

requirements.full_sentence:
The transfer of securities (both Central Government and State Government) to/from HTM
portfolio shall be done as per the policy formulated by the Board.
requirements.object: the policy formulated by the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
Such transfers shall be
permitted only once in a quarter.
requirements.object: in a quarter

requirements.full_sentence:
Transfer of securities to/from HTM portfolio should be done with the approval of the Board
at the acquisition cost/ book value/ market value on the date of transfer, whichever is the
least, and the depreciation, if any, on such transfer shall be fully provided for.
However, it should be ratified by the Board.
requirements.object: Board

requirements.full_sentence:
The quantum of securities that can be classified as HTM shall be restricted to 100% of the
audited NOF of the PD as at the end March of the preceding financial year.
requirements.object: of the audited NOF of the PD as at the end March of the preceding financial year

requirements.full_sentence:
The profit on sale of securities, if any, from the HTM category shall first be taken to the P &
L Account and thereafter be appropriated to the "Reserve Account";
requirements.object: the P & L Account and thereafter be appropriated to the "Reserve Account"

requirements.full_sentence:
loss on sale shall be
recognized in the P & L Account.
requirements.object: P & L Account

requirements.full_sentence:
Investments classified under HTM will be carried at acquisition cost, unless it is more than
the face value, in which case the premium should be amortized over the remaining period
to maturity.
requirements.object: remaining period to maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
The book value of the security should continue to be reduced to the extent of
the amount amortized during the relevant accounting period.
requirements.object: to the extent of the amount amortized during the relevant accounting period

requirements.full_sentence: The concurrent auditors should specifically verify compliance with these instructions.
requirements.object: these instructions

requirements.full_sentence: The facility shall be available until further advice.
requirements.object: advice

requirements.full_sentence:
5.8.2 Banks undertaking PD activities departmentally may continue to follow the extant
guidelines applicable to banks with regard to the classification and valuation of the investment
portfolio issued by Department of Banking Regulation (DBR), RBI.
requirements.object: extant guidelines applicable to banks with regard to the classification and valuation of the investment portfolio issued by Dep

requirements.full_sentence:
6.1 PDs shall have an efficient internal control system for fair conduct of business, settlement of
trades and maintenance of accounts.
requirements.object: control system for fair conduct of business, settlement of trades and maintenance of accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should maintain adequate physical infrastructure and skilled manpower for efficient
participation in primary issues, trading in the secondary market, and to advise and
educate investors.
requirements.object: and skilled manpower for efficient participation in primary issues, trading in the secondary market, and to

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of transactions in G-Sec, a PD should have a separate desk and maintain
separate accounts in respect of its own position and customer transactions and subject
them to external audit also.
requirements.object: and maintain separate accounts in respect of its own position and customer transactions and subject them to external audit also

requirements.full_sentence:
All the transactions put through by the PD either on outright basis or ready forward basis
should be reflected on the same day in its books and records i.e.
requirements.object: same day in its books and records i

requirements.full_sentence:
For every transaction entered into, the trading desk should generate a deal slip which
should contain data relating to nature of the deal, name of the counterparty, whether it is
a direct deal or through a broker, and if through a broker, name of the broker, details of
security, amount, price, contract date and time and settlement date.
requirements.object: which should contain data relating to nature of the deal, name of the counterparty, whether it is a direct deal or through a brok

requirements.full_sentence:
The deal slips
should be serially numbered and controlled separately to ensure that each deal slip has
been properly accounted for.
requirements.object: controlled separately to ensure that each deal slip has been properly accounted for

requirements.full_sentence:
Once the deal is concluded, the deal slip should be
immediately passed on to the back office for recording and processing.
requirements.object: to the back office for recording and processing

requirements.full_sentence:
For each deal,
there must be a system of issue of confirmation to the counter-party.
requirements.object: issue of confirmation to the counter-party

requirements.full_sentence:
Once a deal has been concluded, there should not be any substitution of the counter-
party by the broker.
requirements.object: the counter- party by the broker

requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, the security sold/purchased in a deal should not be
substituted by another security under any circumstances.
requirements.object: security under any circumstances

requirements.full_sentence:
On the basis of vouchers passed by the back office (which should be done after
verification of actual contract notes received from the broker/counter-party and
confirmation of the deal by the counter party), the books of account should be
independently prepared.
requirements.object: of actual contract notes received from the broker/counter-party and confirmation of the deal by the counter party), the boo

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should periodically review securities transactions and report to the top
management, the details of transactions in securities, details of funds/securities delivery
failures, even in cases where shortages have been met by CCIL.
requirements.object: and report to the top management, the details of transactions in securities, details of funds/securities d

requirements.full_sentence:
All security transactions (including transactions on account of clients) should be
subjected to concurrent audit by internal/external auditors to the extent of 100% and the
results of the audit should be placed before the CEO/MD of the PD once every month.
requirements.object: audit by internal/external auditors to the extent of 100% and the results of the audit should be placed before the CEO/MD o

requirements.full_sentence:
The compliance should be monitored on ongoing basis and reported directly to the top
management.
requirements.object: basis and reported directly to the top management

requirements.full_sentence:
The concurrent audit should also cover the business done through
brokers and include the findings in their report.
requirements.object: done through brokers and include the findings in their report

conditions: if any, transactions with related parties on 'arm's length basis'
requirements.full_sentence:
The scope of concurrent audit should include monitoring of broker wise limits, prudential
limits laid down by RBI, accuracy and timely submission of all regulatory returns,
reconciliation of SGL/CSGL balances with PDO statements, reconciliation of current
account balance with DAD statements, settlements through CCIL, stipulations with
respect to short sale deals, when-issued transactions, constituent deals, money market
deals, adherence to accounting standards, verification of deal slips, reasons for
cancellation of deals, if any, transactions with related parties on 'arm's length basis',
provisions related to HTM portfolio etc.
requirements.object: wise limits, prudential limits laid down by RBI, accuracy and timely submission of all regulatory returns, reconciliation of SGL/CSGL balances with PDO statements, reconciliation of current account balance with DAD statements, settlements through CCIL, stipulations with respect to short sale deals

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should have a system of internal audit focused on monitoring the efficacy and
adequacy of internal control systems.
requirements.object: internal audit focused on monitoring the efficacy and adequacy of internal control systems

requirements.full_sentence:
With the approval of their Board, PDs should put in place appropriate exposure limits /
dealing limits, for each of their counterparties which cover all dealings with such counter
parties including money market, repos and outright securities transactions.
requirements.object: exposure limits / dealing limits, for each of their counterparties which cover all dealings with such counter parties includi

requirements.full_sentence:
These limits
should be reviewed periodically on the basis of financial statements, market reports,
ratings, etc.
requirements.object: the basis of financial statements, market reports, ratings, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
With the approval of their Boards, PDs should put in place reasonable leverage ratio for
their operations, which should take into account all outside borrowings as a multiplier of
their NOF.
requirements.object: leverage ratio for their operations, which should take into account all outside borrowings as a multiplier of their NOF

requirements.full_sentence: There should be a clear functional separation of (i) trading (front office);
requirements.object: separation of (i) trading (front office)

requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, there should be a separation of transactions relating to own account and
constituents' accounts.
requirements.object: transactions relating to own account and constituents' accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should have an Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) which should meet at least at
quarterly intervals.
requirements.object: of the Board (ACB) which should meet at least at quarterly intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
The ACB should peruse the findings of the various audits and should
ensure efficacy and adequacy of the audit function.
requirements.object: the various audits and should ensure efficacy and adequacy of the audit function

requirements.full_sentence:
6.2 Purchase/Sale of securities through SGL transfer forms
All PDs should report / conclude their transactions on NDS / NDS-OM and clear/settle them
through CCIL as central counter-party.
requirements.object: transactions on NDS / NDS-OM and clear/settle them through CCIL as central counter-party

conditions:
where exceptions have been permitted to
tender physical SGL transfer forms
requirements.full_sentence:
In such cases where exceptions have been permitted to
tender physical SGL transfer forms, the following guidelines should be followed:
i.

Records of all SGL transfer forms issued/received should be maintained and a system for
verification of the authenticity of the SGL transfer forms received from the counter-party
and confirmation of authorized signatories should be put in place.
requirements.object: system for verification of the authenticity of the SGL transfer forms received from the counter-party and confirmation of authoriz

requirements.full_sentence:
Under no circumstances, a SGL transfer form issued by a PD in favour of counterparty
should bounce for want of sufficient balance in the SGL/Current Account.
requirements.object: sufficient balance in the SGL/Current Account

requirements.full_sentence:
Any instance of
return of SGL form from the Public Debt Office (PDO) of the RBI for want of sufficient
balance in the account should be immediately brought to the notice of the PD's top
management and reported to RBI with the details of transactions.
requirements.object: the notice of the PD's top management and reported to RBI with the details of transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
SGL Transfer forms received by purchasing PDs should be deposited in their SGL
Accounts immediately.
requirements.object: SGL Accounts immediately

requirements.full_sentence:
No sale should be effected by way of return of SGL form held by
the PD.
requirements.object: of return of SGL form held by the PD

requirements.full_sentence:
SGL transfer form should be in a standard format, prescribed by the RBI, and printed on
semi-security paper of uniform size.
requirements.object: format, prescribed by the RBI, and printed on semi-security paper of uniform size

requirements.full_sentence:
They should be serially numbered and there should
be a control system in place to account for each SGL form.
requirements.object: there should be a control system in place to account for each SGL form

requirements.full_sentence:
6.3 Bank Receipt or similar receipt should not be issued or accepted by the PDs under any
circumstances in respect of transactions in G-Sec.
requirements.object: by the PDs under any circumstances in respect of transactions in G-Sec

requirements.full_sentence:
6.4 Reconciliation of holdings of G-Sec
Balances as per books of PDs should be reconciled at least at monthly intervals with the
balances in the books of PDOs.
requirements.object: at monthly intervals with the balances in the books of PDOs

conditions: If the number of transactions so warrant
requirements.full_sentence:
If the number of transactions so warrant, the reconciliation
should be undertaken at more frequent intervals.
requirements.object: frequent intervals

requirements.full_sentence:
This reconciliation should be periodically
verified during concurrent/internal audit of the PDs.
requirements.object: concurrent/internal audit of the PDs

requirements.full_sentence:
The PDs should undertake all due diligence while acting as agent of their clients for
carrying out transactions in securities.
requirements.object: while acting as agent of their clients for carrying out transactions in securities

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should not use the constituents' funds or assets for proprietary trading or for financing
of another intermediary's operations.
requirements.object: or assets for proprietary trading or for financing of another intermediary's operations

requirements.full_sentence:
All transaction records should give a clear indication that the transaction belongs to
constituent and does not belong to PD's own account.
requirements.object: that the transaction belongs to constituent and does not belong to PD's own account

requirements.full_sentence:
The transactions on behalf of constituents and the operations in the CSGL accounts
should be conducted in accordance with the guidelines issued by RBI on the CSGL
accounts.
requirements.object: with the guidelines issued by RBI on the CSGL accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
CSGL account holders) and offer the facility of maintaining
gilt accounts to their constituents, should not permit settlement of any sale transaction by
their constituents unless the security sold is actually held in the gilt account of the
constituent.
requirements.object: sale transaction by their constituents unless the security sold is actually held in the gilt account of the constituent

requirements.full_sentence:
Indirect access to NDS-OM has been permitted to certain segments of investors through
banks and PDs vide circular IDMD.DOD.No.5893/10.25.66/2007-08 dated May 27, 2008.

PDs should adhere to the guidelines on maintenance of gilt accounts and investments on
behalf of gilt account holders while undertaking 'constituent deals' on NDS-OM.
requirements.object: on maintenance of gilt accounts and investments on behalf of gilt account holders while undertaking 'constituent deals' on ND

conditions:
if completed through the
securities/funds shortage handling procedure of CCIL
requirements.full_sentence:
A report on such transaction, even if completed through the
securities/funds shortage handling procedure of CCIL, must be submitted to the IDMD, RBI
immediately.
requirements.object: IDMD, RBI immediately

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of circular RBI/2010-11/115-IDMD.DOD.17/11.01.01(B)/2010-11 dated
July 14, 2010, for any default in delivery of security / funds in a SGL sale / purchase transaction
undertaken by a PD (event of bouncing of SGL transfer forms) and the failure of the PD to offer
satisfactory explanation for such bouncing of SGL, the PD shall be liable to pay penalties as per
the circular ibid.
requirements.object: penalties as per the circular ibid

requirements.full_sentence:
Violation/Circumvention of Instructions
Any violation/circumvention of the above guidelines or the terms and conditions of the
undertaking executed by a PD with RBI (Annex I) would be viewed seriously and such violation
would attract penal action including the withdrawal of liquidity support, denial of access to the
money market, withdrawal of authorization for carrying on the business as a PD, and/or
imposition of monetary penalty or liquidated damages, as the RBI may deem fit.

8.1 In order to maintain transparency with regard to imposition of penalties and in conformity
with the best practices on disclosure of penalties imposed by the regulator, the details of the
penalty levied on a PD shall be placed in the public domain.
requirements.object: public domain

requirements.full_sentence:
The penalty shall also be disclosed in the 'Notes on Accounts' to the Balance Sheet of the
PD in its next Annual Report.
requirements.object: the 'Notes on Accounts' to the Balance Sheet of the PD in its next Annual Report

requirements.full_sentence:
9.1 RBI may suspend or terminate a PD, as it may deem fit, in the following circumstances:
i.
requirements.object: PD, as it may deem fit, in the following circumstances: i

requirements.full_sentence:
In such a case, the PD should
discuss the orderly unwinding of any positions held, agree the timing and date of
termination of PD operations, and the content of any announcements to be made to the
market, by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: of any positions held, agree the timing and date of termination of PD operations, and the content of any announcements t

requirements.full_sentence:
An
announcement in this regard shall be made preferably on the last working day of the week, with
the sanction effective from the close of business on the same day.
requirements.object: the last working day of the week, with the sanction effective from the close of business on the same day

requirements.full_sentence:
2.1 Banks eligible to apply for undertaking PD business, [please see eligibility conditions at
paragraph 1.2.1 of Section I above] may approach the Chief General Manager, IDMD, RBI, 23rd
Floor, Central Office Building, Fort, Mumbai - 400 001 for an authorization for undertaking PD
business departmentally.
requirements.object: Manager, IDMD, RBI, 23rd Floor, Central Office Building, Fort, Mumbai - 400 001 for an authorization for undertaking PD bus

requirements.full_sentence:
3.3 The requirement of ensuring minimum investment in G-Sec and T-Bills on a daily basis,
based on net call / RBI borrowing and NOF will not be applicable to bank-PDs who shall be
guided by the extant guidelines applicable to banks.
requirements.object: extant guidelines applicable to banks

requirements.full_sentence:
3.5 The guidelines issued vide circular IDMD.No/3426/11.01.01 (D)/2005-06 dated May 3, 2006,
as updated vide circulars IDMD.No/2130/11/01.01 (D)/2006-07 dated November 16, 2006,
IDMD.DOD.No.3166/11/01.01(B)/2007-08 dated January 1, 2008 and
FMRD.DIRD.06/14.03.07/2015-16 dated December 10, 2015 on 'When-issued' trades will be
applicable to bank-PDs also.
requirements.object: vide circulars IDMD

requirements.full_sentence:
3.6 Bank-PDs shall be guided by the extant guidelines applicable to banks as regards borrowing
in call/notice/term money market, ICDs, FCNR (B) loans /External Commercial Borrowings and
other sources of funds.
requirements.object: extant guidelines applicable to banks as regards borrowing in call/notice/term money market, ICDs, FCNR (B) loans /External Commercia

requirements.full_sentence: 3.7 The investment policy of the bank may be suitably amended to include PD activities also.
requirements.object: include PD activities also

requirements.full_sentence:
3.10 Bank-PDs shall be guided by the extant guidelines applicable to banks as regards
business through brokers, repo transactions, interest rate derivatives (OTC & exchange traded),
investment in non-G-Sec, Issue of Subordinated Debt Instruments and declaration of dividend.
requirements.object: extant guidelines applicable to banks as regards business through brokers, repo transactions, interest rate derivatives (OTC & exchan

requirements.full_sentence:
4.1 The transactions related to PD business, undertaken by a bank departmentally, should be
executed through the existing SGL account of the bank.
requirements.object: existing SGL account of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
However, banks may clearly indicate in their investment policy the intention of
denoting the whole or part of HFT-G-Sec book as PD book with the approval of their
Board/ALCO.
requirements.object: investment policy the intention of denoting the whole or part of HFT-G-Sec book as PD book with the approval of their Board/

requirements.full_sentence:
4.2 Bank-PDs shall decide about classification (HTM/AFS/HFT-G-Sec-bank/HFT-G-Sec-PD) of
securities allotted in the primary auction/purchased in the secondary market, at the time of
acquisition of the securities.
requirements.object: of securities allotted in the primary auction/purchased in the secondary market, at

requirements.full_sentence:
4.3 It should be ensured that, at any point of time, there is a minimum balance of Rs.100 crore
of G-Sec earmarked for PD business.
requirements.object: any point of time, there is a minimum balance of Rs

requirements.full_sentence:
4.4 Bank-PDs should subject 100 per cent of the transactions and regulatory returns submitted
by PD department to concurrent audit.
requirements.object: of the transactions and regulatory returns submitted by PD department to concurrent audit

requirements.full_sentence:
An auditor's certificate for having maintained the
minimum stipulated balance of Rs.100 crore of G-Sec in the PD-book on an ongoing basis and
having adhered to the guidelines/instructions issued by RBI should be forwarded to IDMD, RBI
on a quarterly basis.
requirements.object: RBI on a quarterly basis

requirements.full_sentence:
In other words,
for the purpose of assessing the bank's capital adequacy requirement and coverage under risk
management framework, the PD activity should also be taken into account.
requirements.object: account

requirements.full_sentence:
5.2 The bank undertaking PD activity may put in place adequate risk management systems to
measure and provide for the risks emanating from the PD activity.
requirements.object: risk management systems to measure and provide for the risks emanating from the PD activity

requirements.full_sentence:
6.1 The banks authorized to undertake PD business departmentally are required to submit
prescribed periodic returns to RBI promptly.
requirements.object: returns to RBI promptly

requirements.full_sentence:
6.2 Reserve Bank reserves its right to amend or modify the above guidelines from time to time,
as may be considered necessary.
AND WHEREAS as a precondition to our being authorized to undertake PD activity we are required to furnish an undertaking covering the relative terms and conditions.
requirements.object: covering the relative terms and conditions

requirements.full_sentence: To commit to aggregatively bid in the auction of Treasury Bills (TBs), including Cash Management Bills (CMBs) to the extent of specific per cent of each issue of auction Treasury Bills/Cash Management Bills as decided every year (April-March) and for a minimum amount equal to the underwriting commitment (allotted under Minimum Underwriting Commitment and Additional Competitive Underwriting) for GoI Dated Securities and to maintain the success ratio in aggregate winning bids at not less than 40 per cent for TBs and CMBs or such other percent as may be notified by RBI keeping in view the relevant factors.
requirements.object: keeping in view the relevant factors

requirements.full_sentence: To offer to underwrite primary issues of GoI dated securities, TBs, CMBs, and State Government securities, for which auction is held, and accept devolvement, if any, of any amount as may be determined by RBI in terms of prevalent scheme for Bidding/Underwriting.
requirements.object: in terms of prevalent scheme for Bidding/Underwriting

requirements.full_sentence: To achieve an annual turnover of not less than 5 times in Government dated securities and not less than 10 times in TBs/CMBs of the average of month-end stocks (in the book separately maintained for the PD business) subject to the turnover in respect of outright transactions being not less than 3 times in Government dated securities and 6 times in TBs /CMBs or as may be notified by RBI from time to time.
requirements.object: from time to time

requirements.full_sentence: To maintain the capital adequacy standards prescribed by the RBI, and to subject ourselves to all prudential and regulatory guidelines as may be issued by the RBI from time to time.
requirements.object: RBI from time to time

requirements.full_sentence: To comply with all applicable RBI /Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) requirements under the existing guidelines and which may be laid down from time to time in this behalf, failing which RBI would be at liberty to cancel the authorisation as a PD.
requirements.object: time to time in this behalf, failing which RBI would be at liberty to cancel the authorisation as a PD

requirements.full_sentence: To maintain separate books of account for transactions relating to PD business (distinct from the normal banking business) with necessary audit trails and to ensure that, at any point of time, there is a minimum balance of Rs.100 crore of Government securities or as may be notified by RBI from time to time earmarked for PD business (applicable to bank-PDs only).
requirements.object: from time to time earmarked for PD business (applicable to bank-PDs only)

requirements.full_sentence: To maintain and preserve such information, records, books and documents pertaining to our working as a PD as may be specified by the RBI from time to time.
requirements.object: RBI from time to time

requirements.full_sentence: To maintain at all times a minimum net owned funds of Rs.150 crore / Rs.250 crore or as may be prescribed by RBI from time to time in Government securities and to deploy the liquidity support from the RBI, net borrowings from call money market and net repo borrowings exclusively in Government securities (applicable to standalone PDs only).
requirements.object: from time to time in Government securities and to deploy the liquidity support from the RBI, net borrowings from call money marke

requirements.full_sentence: To submit in prescribed formats periodic reports including daily transactions and market information, monthly report of details of transactions in securities and risk position and performance with regard to participation in auctions, annual audited accounts and an annual performance review and such statements, certificates and other documents and information as may be specified by RBI from time to time.
requirements.object: from time to time

requirements.full_sentence: Note: The last date prescribed for submission of these statements by the departments concerned and/or IDMD should be adhered to.
# Board approved figures may be given in the foot note.
requirements.object: foot note

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs are required to adhere to the guidelines on activities
permitted to be undertaken by PDs while diversifying business activities.
requirements.object: guidelines on activities permitted to be undertaken by PDs while diversifying business activities

conditions: In case where the guarantee has been invoked and the concerned state government has remained in default
requirements.full_sentence:
Investments
Note: In case where the guarantee has been invoked and the concerned state government has remained in default, PDs should assign 100% risk weight.

Foreign Exchange Contract*


Residual maturity of 1 year or less

2


Residual maturity of 1 year to 5 years

10


Residual maturity of over 5 years

15


Note: Cash margins/deposits should be deducted before applying the credit conversion factor
*:Risk weights would be as per the counterparty

BB.
requirements.object: the credit conversion factor *:Risk weights would be as per the counterparty BB

requirements.full_sentence:
250


The daily VaR preceding holidays should be up-scaled by the square root of number of intervening holidays.
requirements.object: square root of number of intervening holidays

conditions: if the Friday is followed by 2 holidays
requirements.full_sentence: For, example, if the Friday is followed by 2 holidays, then the one VaR figure for Friday should be multiplied by square root of 2.
requirements.object: root of 2

requirements.full_sentence:
Duration of NOF (Dn) = (Va*Da - Vl*Dl)/(Va-Vl)
Percentage change in NOF = (-) Dn*Change in interest rates (1%)
Change in NOF = (-) Dn* Change in Interest rates (1%)*NOF
Other details:
Net interest income in the current year so far Trading profits/loss in the current year so far Unrealised MTM (Net gain/loss on cash positions) Unrealised MTM (Net gain/loss on derivative positions) Other income, if any (Details to be specified) *** NOF deployed in fixed income and related instruments Total NOF (Break-up to be furnished)
Note: NOF should be determined as per the definition prescribed in this regard.
requirements.object: the definition prescribed in this regard

requirements.full_sentence: The MTM gains or losses should be adjusted in the NOF.
requirements.object: NOF
- the PD's risk management system is conceptually sound and its implementation is certified by external auditors

requirements.full_sentence:
The qualitative criteria include:
a) A PD should have an independent risk control unit that is responsible for the design and
implementation of the system.
requirements.object: control unit that is responsible for the design and implementation of the system

requirements.full_sentence:
The unit should produce and analyze daily reports on the
output of the PD's risk measurement model, including an evaluation of the relationship
between measures of risk exposure and trading limits.
requirements.object: reports on the output of the PD's risk measurement model, including an evaluation of the relationship between measures of ri

requirements.full_sentence:
This unit must be independent from
trading desks and should report directly to senior management.
requirements.object: desks and should report directly to senior management

requirements.full_sentence: b) The unit should conduct a regular back testing programme, i.e.
requirements.object: testing programme, i

requirements.full_sentence:
c) Board and senior management should be actively involved in the risk control process and
must regard risk control as an essential aspect of the business to which significant resources
need to be devoted.
requirements.object: the risk control process and must regard risk control as an essential aspect of the business to which significant resources ne

requirements.full_sentence:
The daily reports prepared by the independent risk control unit must be
reviewed by a level of management with sufficient seniority and authority to enforce both
reductions in positions taken by individual traders and reductions in the PD's overall risk
exposure.
requirements.object: level of management with sufficient seniority and authority to enforce both reductions in positions taken by individual traders and r

requirements.full_sentence:
d) The PD's internal risk measurement model must be closely integrated into the day-to-day
risk management process of the institution.
requirements.object: the day-to-day risk management process of the institution

requirements.full_sentence:
Its output should accordingly be an integral part
of the process of planning, monitoring and controlling the PD's market risk profile.
requirements.object: part of the process of planning, monitoring and controlling the PD's market risk profile

requirements.full_sentence:
e) The risk measurement system should be used in conjunction with internal trading and
exposure limits.
requirements.object: with internal trading and exposure limits

requirements.full_sentence:
Trading limits should be related to the PD's risk measurement model in
a manner that is consistent over time and that it is well-understood by both traders and
senior management.
requirements.object: PD's risk measurement model in a manner that is consistent over time and that it is well-understood by both traders and senior manag

requirements.full_sentence:
f) A routine and rigorous programme of stress testing should be in place as a supplement
to the risk analysis based on the day-to-day output of the PD's risk measurement model.
requirements.object: a supplement to the risk analysis based on the day-to-day output of the PD's risk measurement model

requirements.full_sentence:
The results of stress testing should be reviewed periodically by senior management and
reflected in the policies and limits set by management and the Board.
requirements.object: senior management and reflected in the policies and limits set by management and the Board

conditions:
Where stress
tests reveal particular vulnerability to a given set of circumstances
requirements.full_sentence:
Where stress
tests reveal particular vulnerability to a given set of circumstances, prompt steps should
be taken to manage those risks appropriately.
requirements.object: those risks appropriately

requirements.full_sentence:
g) PDs should have a routine in place for ensuring compliance with a documented set of
internal policies, controls and procedures concerning the operation of the risk
measurement system.
requirements.object: place for ensuring compliance with a documented set of internal policies, controls and procedures concerning the operation of the ri

requirements.full_sentence:
The risk measurement system must be well documented, for
example, through a manual that describes the basic principles of the risk management
system and that provides an explanation of the empirical techniques used to measure
market risk.
requirements.object: example, through a manual that describes the basic principles of the risk management system and that provides an explanation o

requirements.full_sentence:
h) An independent review of the risk measurement system should be carried out regularly
in the PD's own internal auditing process.
requirements.object: in the PD's own internal auditing process

requirements.full_sentence:
This review should include the activities of the
trading desks as well as the risk control unit.
requirements.object: the trading desks as well as the risk control unit

requirements.full_sentence:
A review of the overall risk management
process should take place at regular intervals (ideally not less than once a year) and
should specifically address, at a minimum:
• the adequacy of the documentation of the risk management system and process;
requirements.object: intervals (ideally not less than once a year) and should specifically address, at a minimum: • the adequacy of the documentation

requirements.full_sentence:
i) The integrity and implementation of the risk management system in accordance with the
system policies/procedures laid down by the Board should be certified by the external
auditors as outlined at Para B.5.
requirements.object: external auditors as outlined at Para B

requirements.full_sentence: j) A copy of the back testing result should be furnished to RBI.

The risk factors contained in a market risk measurement system should
be sufficient to capture the risks inherent in the entire portfolio of the PD.
requirements.object: the risks inherent in the entire portfolio of the PD

requirements.full_sentence:
The following
guidelines should be kept in view:
a) For interest rates, there must be a set of risk factors corresponding to interest rates in each
portfolio in which the PD has interest-rate-sensitive on-or-off-balance sheet positions.
requirements.object: a) For interest rates, there must be a set of risk factors corresponding to interest rates in each portfolio in which the PD has inte

requirements.full_sentence:
The
risk measurement system should model the yield curve using one of a number of generally
accepted approaches, for example, by estimating forward rates of zero coupon yields.
requirements.object: using one of a number of generally accepted approaches, for example, by estimating forward rates of zero coupon yields

requirements.full_sentence:
The
yield curve should be divided into various maturity segments in order to capture variation in
the volatility of rates along the yield curve.
requirements.object: maturity segments in order to capture variation in the volatility of rates along the yield curve

requirements.full_sentence:
For material exposures to interest rate
movements in the major instruments, PDs must model the yield curve using all material risk
factors, driven by the nature of the PD's trading strategies.
requirements.object: using all material risk factors, driven by the nature of the PD's trading strategies

requirements.full_sentence:
The risk measurement system must incorporate separate risk factors to
capture spread risk (e.g.
requirements.object: to capture spread risk (e

requirements.full_sentence:
b) For equity prices, at a minimum, there should be a risk factor that is designed to capture
market-wide movements in equity prices (e.g.
requirements.object: that is designed to capture market-wide movements in equity prices (e

requirements.full_sentence:
B.3 Quantitative standards
a) PDs should update their data sets at least once every three months and should also
reassess them whenever market prices are subject to material changes.
requirements.object: at least once every three months and should also reassess them whenever market prices are subject to material changes

requirements.full_sentence:
RBI may also
require PDs to calculate their VaR using a shorter observation period if, in its judgement,
this is justified by a significant upsurge in price volatility.
requirements.object: calculate their VaR using a shorter observation period if, in its judgement, this is justified by a significant upsurge in price v

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs that use the internal models approach for meeting market risk capital requirements
must have in place a rigorous and comprehensive stress testing program to identify events or
influences that could greatly impact them.
requirements.object: rigorous and comprehensive stress testing program to identify events or influences that could greatly impact them

requirements.full_sentence:
Stress test of PDs should be both of a quantitative and qualitative nature, incorporating both
market risk and liquidity aspects of market disturbances.
requirements.object: quantitative and qualitative nature, incorporating both market risk and liquidity aspects of market disturbances

requirements.full_sentence:
Quantitative criteria should identify
plausible stress scenarios to which PDs could be exposed.
requirements.object: to which PDs could be exposed

requirements.full_sentence:
Qualitative criteria should
emphasize that two major goals of stress testing are to evaluate the capacity of the PD's capital
to absorb potential large losses and to identify steps the PD can take to reduce its risk and
conserve capital.
requirements.object: goals of stress testing are to evaluate the capacity of the PD's capital to absorb potential large losses and to identify ste

requirements.full_sentence:
This assessment is integral to setting and evaluating the PD's management
strategy and the results of stress testing should be regularly communicated to senior
management and, periodically, to the Board of the PD.
requirements.object: senior management and, periodically, to the Board of the PD

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should combine the standard stress scenarios with stress tests developed by PDs
themselves to reflect their specific risk characteristics.
requirements.object: scenarios with stress tests developed by PDs themselves to reflect their specific risk characteristics

requirements.full_sentence:
Specifically, RBI may ask PDs to provide
information on stress testing in three broad areas as discussed below.
requirements.object: information on stress testing in three broad areas as discussed below

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) Scenarios requiring no simulations by a PD
PDs should have information on the largest losses experienced during the reporting period
available for RBI's review.
requirements.object: largest losses experienced during the reporting period available for RBI's review

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Scenarios requiring a simulation by a PD
PDs should subject their portfolios to a series of simulated stress scenarios and provide RBI
with the results.
requirements.object: a series of simulated stress scenarios and provide RBI with the results

requirements.full_sentence:
Due consideration should be
given to the sharp variation that at times has occurred in a matter of days in periods of
significant market disturbance.
requirements.object: sharp variation that at times has occurred in a matter of days in periods of significant market disturbance

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) Scenarios developed by a PD to capture the specific characteristics of its portfolio
In addition to the scenarios prescribed by RBI under (a) and (b) above, a PD should also
develop its own stress tests which it identified as most adverse based on the characteristics
of its portfolio.
requirements.object: stress tests which it identified as most adverse based on the characteristics of its portfolio

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should provide RBI with a description of the methodology used to identify
and carry out stress testing under the scenarios, as well as with a description of the results
derived from these scenarios.
requirements.object: description of the methodology used to identify and carry out stress testing under the scenarios, as well as with a description of

requirements.full_sentence:
The results should be reviewed periodically by senior management and should be reflected in
the policies and limits set by management and the Board.
requirements.object: senior management and should be reflected in the policies and limits set by management and the Board

requirements.full_sentence:
B.5 External Validation
PDs should get the internal model validated by external auditors, including at a minimum, the
following:
requirements.object: validated by external auditors, including at a minimum, the following:

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Ensuring that the formulae used in the calculation process as well as for the pricing of
complex instruments are validated by a qualified unit, which in all cases should be
independent from the trading desks.
requirements.object: trading desks

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs should make the results as well as the
underlying inputs to their VaR calculations available to the external auditors.
requirements.object: well as the underlying inputs to their VaR calculations available to the external auditors

conditions: If the comparison uncovers sufficient differences
requirements.full_sentence:
If the comparison uncovers sufficient differences, there may be problems, either
with the model or with the assumptions of the back test.
requirements.object: the model or with the assumptions of the back test

requirements.full_sentence:
Comparing the risk
measures with the trading outcomes simply means that the PD counts the number of times that
the risk measures were larger than the trading outcome.
requirements.object: the number of times that the risk measures were larger than the trading outcome

requirements.full_sentence:
3.4 PDs should perform back tests based on the hypothetical changes in portfolio value that
would occur;
requirements.object: on the hypothetical changes in portfolio value that would occur

requirements.full_sentence: 3.6 PDs should perform back tests using both hypothetical and actual trading outcomes.
requirements.object: both hypothetical and actual trading outcomes

requirements.full_sentence:
For example, over 200 trading days, a 99% daily risk
measure should cover, on average, 198 of the 200 trading outcomes, leaving two exceptions.
requirements.object: of the 200 trading outcomes, leaving two exceptions

requirements.full_sentence:
PDs may
however base the back test on as many observations as possible.
requirements.object: test on as many observations as possible

requirements.full_sentence:
Nevertheless, the most
recent 250 trading days' observations should be used for the purposes of back testing.
requirements.object: purposes of back testing

requirements.full_sentence:
3.8 Normally, in view of the 99% confidence level adopted, 2.5 exceptions may be
acceptable in the observation period of 250 days.
requirements.object: observation period of 250 days

requirements.full_sentence:
Depending on the number of exceptions generated by the PD's
back testing model, both actual as well as hypothetical, RBI may initiate a dialogue regarding
the PD's model, enhance the multiplication factor, may impose an increase in the capital
requirement or disallow use of the model as indicated above depending on the number of
exceptions.
requirements.object: the PD's model, enhance the multiplication factor, may impose an increase in the capital requirement or disallow use of

conditions: In case large number of exceptions is being noticed
requirements.full_sentence:
3.9 In case large number of exceptions is being noticed, it may be useful for the PDs to dis-
aggregate their activities into sub sectors in order to identify the large exceptions on their own.
requirements.object: PDs to dis- aggregate their activities into sub sectors in order to identify the large exceptions on their own

requirements.full_sentence:
the
computer is dividing by 250 when it should be dividing by 225).

Where average figures are involved, it may be taken to mean as average of

 month end balances.
requirements.object: as average of month end balances

requirements.full_sentence: PV01 may be taken as POSITIVE for long positions and NEGATIVE for short positions.
requirements.object: for long positions and NEGATIVE for short positions

requirements.full_sentence: PV01 may be taken as POSITIVE if receiving a swap and NEGATIVE if paying a swap.
requirements.object: if receiving a swap and NEGATIVE if paying a swap

requirements.full_sentence: In 2 (e) above, swaps on other benchmarks such as LIBOR may be reported separately for each benchmark
requirements.object: each benchmark
